description_long: This is an ornate stone grave monument featuring a rectangular base with a slightly protruding plaque area on one side, topped by a domed lid adorned with four sculpted corner finials. The monument is heavily weathered, with patches of green moss and lichen covering much of its surface, especially on the top and upper sides. At the very top sits a decorative urn, also made of stone, which is intricately carved and shows signs of aging. The base is surrounded by a platform that appears to be set into the ground, with fallen leaves and earth visible around it. The front face of the monument displays a plaque with faint, partially legible inscriptions, while the other sides are plainer, with some geometric detailing. The overall appearance suggests a historical or Victorian-era grave marker, likely found in an old cemetery or park.
description: A weathered stone grave monument with a domed lid, decorative urn, and moss-covered surfaces, featuring a plaque with faint inscriptions on the front.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the back of the monument, with heavy moss and lichen growth and no visible inscriptions.
description_view_1: The front view displays the main plaque area with faint inscriptions, framed by moss and decorative stonework.
description_view_2: The left side view highlights the monument's rectangular shape, mossy surface, and the urn on top, with minimal detailing.
description_view_3: The right side view shows a mostly plain stone surface with some geometric detailing and significant weathering.
